evidence: line 79: TOTAL_DURATION=$(node -e "console.log(JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('$TIMELINE','utf8')).totalDuration)") splices $TIMELINE unescaped into a single-quoted JS string
description: Raw --timeline= CLI arg is spliced unescaped into a node -e JS string; a path containing a quote breaks out and injects arbitrary JS
suggested_fix: Pass TIMELINE to node via argv/env instead of string interpolation, e.g. node -e "..." -- "$TIMELINE" and read process.argv

evidence: line 80: VOICEOVER_REL=$(node -e "console.log(JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('$TIMELINE','utf8')).voiceover || 'voiceover.mp3')") repeats the same unescaped interpolation
description: Second unescaped $TIMELINE interpolation into a node -e JS string reading the voiceover path
suggested_fix: Pass TIMELINE via argv/env instead of string interpolation

evidence: line 89: RECORD_DURATION=$(node -e "console.log(Math.ceil($TOTAL_DURATION + 1))") re-interpolates the line-79 output unescaped
description: $TOTAL_DURATION, reachable via the line-79 injection chain, is re-interpolated unescaped into a further node -e arithmetic expression
suggested_fix: Pass TOTAL_DURATION via argv/env instead of string interpolation

evidence: lines 109-111 and 136-139: wget/curl fetch https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh to /tmp then chmod +x and execute it directly, repeated in the apt branch and the generic fallback branch
description: Downloads Microsoft's dotnet-install.sh and executes it with no checksum or signature verification
suggested_fix: Verify a published checksum/signature of the installer before executing it, or vendor a pinned copy

evidence: lines 112-113 and 140-141: export PATH="$HOME/.dotnet:$PATH" then echo the same line appended to ~/.bashrc, repeated in both install branches
description: Prepends a user-writable directory to PATH and permanently mutates ~/.bashrc outside the repo
suggested_fix: Scope the PATH change to the current script/session only; avoid silently editing the user's shell rc file

evidence: lines 107,108,117,120,123: sudo apt-get/dnf/pacman/zypper install run unprompted in every package-manager branch
description: Runs sudo package installs unprompted across every supported package manager branch
suggested_fix: Prompt for confirmation before invoking sudo, or document this clearly as a requirement up front

evidence: find_ws_path() returns os.path.join(work_dir, 'xl', rel.get('Target')) with rel.get('Target') taken verbatim from workbook.xml.rels, no '../' sanitization, code read and confirmed no bounds check
description: Unsanitized rels Target from workbook.xml.rels is joined into a filesystem path and then opened/overwritten, a zip-slip-style vulnerability on a crafted .xlsx
suggested_fix: Resolve the joined path and verify it stays within work_dir before opening/writing it

evidence: find_ws_path() repeats the identical unsanitized os.path.join(work_dir, 'xl', rel.get('Target')) pattern
description: Identical unsanitized rels Target path-join pattern as xlsx_add_column.py, zip-slip on a crafted .xlsx
suggested_fix: Resolve the joined path and verify it stays within work_dir before opening/writing it

evidence: _load_from_dir() builds os.path.join(unpacked_dir, 'xl', rel_path); substring/startswith('worksheets/') checks do not block '../' segments; code read and confirmed no bounds check
description: Unsanitized rels Target allows os.path.exists/open to read arbitrary files outside the unpacked directory into the audit report
suggested_fix: Resolve the joined path and verify it stays within unpacked_dir before reading it

evidence: line 29 and line 64: urllib.request.urlopen(req, ...) to commons.wikimedia.org
description: Outbound HTTP(S) calls to commons.wikimedia.org to fetch image assets
suggested_fix: No action required if wikimedia.org is an intended, trusted dependency; document the network dependency

evidence: line 1097: tmpDir = process.env.TMPDIR || '/tmp'
description: Reads TMPDIR from environment to configure a Playwright temp directory
suggested_fix: Validate TMPDIR points to a writable directory before use, or document expected values

evidence: lines 109 and 136: wget/curl fetch of https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh
description: Network fetch of the dotnet installer script, the download leg preceding the SEC-download-execute finding
suggested_fix: Verify a checksum/signature of the downloaded script before executing it

evidence: ensure_deps() in fill_inspect.py:27, fill_write.py:36, merge.py:21, reformat_parse.py:42, render_body.py:44 each call subprocess.check_call to pip install pypdf/reportlab at runtime if missing
description: Five minimax-pdf scripts each install their own Python dependency (pypdf/reportlab) via pip at runtime if the import is missing
suggested_fix: Declare dependencies in a requirements file installed ahead of time rather than at runtime

evidence: cmd_fix installs reportlab, pypdf, matplotlib via pip --break-system-packages at runtime
description: cmd_fix installs multiple Python packages via pip at runtime using --break-system-packages
suggested_fix: Declare dependencies in a requirements file installed ahead of time rather than at runtime

evidence: cmd_fix runs 'npm install -g playwright' then 'npx playwright install chromium'; render_cover.js:62 runs the same auto-recovery install
description: Runtime install of the Playwright package globally plus a Chromium binary download
suggested_fix: Pin and vendor Playwright/Chromium as an explicit setup step rather than a runtime auto-fix

evidence: spawnSync("npx", ["playwright","install","chromium"], { stdio: "inherit", shell: true }); args are static literals, not attacker-controlled
description: Subprocess spawned with shell:true; args are currently static so exploitability is low, but the construct is a risky pattern
suggested_fix: Remove shell:true since the args array does not require shell interpretation

evidence: line 23: CSS @import url() to fonts.googleapis.com; lines 996,1097,1378: where img_url comes from tokens['cover_image'] (ultimately --cover-image CLI arg), fetched by the headless browser at render time; palette.py:229 hardcodes further fonts.googleapis.com URLs
description: Embeds Google Fonts CSS plus an unsanitized user-supplied cover_image URL into generated HTML, both fetched by a headless browser at render time
suggested_fix: Restrict cover_image to http(s)/local-file schemes and reject link-local/metadata IP ranges before rendering

evidence: line 35: reads PLANNING_DISABLED from environment and exits immediately (bypassing the completion gate) when it equals "1"; line 183 also reads PWF_GATE_CAP; resolve-plan-dir.sh:109 reads PLAN_ID; init-session.sh:93 reads PYTHON_BIN; session-catchup.py reads CODEX_THREAD_ID (128), CODEX_SESSIONS_DIR (153), XDG_DATA_HOME/OPENCODE_DATA_DIR (189)
description: Multiple planning-with-files scripts read config/control values from environment variables, including one (PLANNING_DISABLED) that silently bypasses the completion gate
suggested_fix: Log a visible warning whenever the gate is bypassed via PLANNING_DISABLED; validate the other env-sourced paths/values before use

evidence: local test_output="/tmp/minimax-docx-setup-test-$$.docx", a predictable filename guarded only by the shell PID
description: Verification test document written to a predictable /tmp filename based only on the shell PID
suggested_fix: Use mktemp to generate the test output path instead of a PID-suffixed literal

evidence: PLAN_DIR="${PLAN_ROOT}/${PLAN_ID}" built directly from the first CLI arg with no slug_is_valid/containment guard, unlike resolve-plan-dir.sh
description: PLAN_ID is joined into a path with no slug validation, unlike the equivalent guard in resolve-plan-dir.sh
suggested_fix: Reuse resolve-plan-dir.sh's slug_is_valid + containment check before constructing PLAN_DIR
